tracks.rawText: 2:36Rod Stewart - Da Ya Think I'm Sexy ？ (Edward Newgate Remix) [zuguIAvRpzE]v5.5CoverHigh-energy nu-disco and dance-pop at 120 BPM in B minor, starting full-throttle with a punchy four-on-the-floor kick, crisp backbeat snare, prominent slap bass, and octave-jumping bassline, Syncopated electric guitar scratches, bright poly synth stabs/fills, tight female vocals with light compression and subtle doubling, plus shakers and tambourines, Wah-wah guitar bridge2PublishRemix
tracks.title: Rod Stewart - Da Ya Think I'm Sexy ？ (Edward Newgate Remix) [zuguIAvRpzE]

tracks.rawText: 3:08Rod Stewart - Da Ya Think I'm Sexy ？ (Edward Newgate Remix) [zuguIAvRpzE]v5.5CoverHigh-energy nu-disco and dance-pop at 120 BPM in B minor, starting full-throttle with a punchy four-on-the-floor kick, crisp backbeat snare, prominent slap bass, and octave-jumping bassline, Syncopated electric guitar scratches, bright poly synth stabs/fills, tight female vocals with light compression and subtle doubling, plus shakers and tambourines, Wah-wah guitar bridge1PublishRemix
tracks.title: Rod Stewart - Da Ya Think I'm Sexy ？ (Edward Newgate Remix) [zuguIAvRpzE]

tracks.rawText: 3:07Rod Stewart - Da Ya Think I'm Sexy ？ (Edward Newgate Remix) [zuguIAvRpzE]v5.5CoverNu-disco and dance-pop track featuring a prominent slap bass line and syncopated electric guitar scratches, The arrangement is built around a four-on-the-floor kick drum and a crisp snare on the backbeat, A bright, polyphonic synthesizer provides rhythmic stabs and melodic fills, Female vocals are processed with light compression and subtle doubling, The track maintains a steady 120 BPM in the key of B minor, The bassline uses a repetitive octave-jumping pattern, while the guitar employs a wah-wah effect during the bridge, High-frequency percussion, including shakers and tambourines, adds rhythmic density to the high endPublishRemix
tracks.title: Rod Stewart - Da Ya Think I'm Sexy ？ (Edward Newgate Remix) [zuguIAvRpzE]

tracks.rawText: 1:33Da Ya Think I'm Sexyv5.5CoverRock metal dub with slap bass locked to a four-on-the-floor kick, syncopated palm-muted electric guitars, and huge dub delay throws on snare and guitar hits, Female vocals ride bright synth stabs, backbeat claps, and wide stereo reverb; outro drops to bass and drums only with tape echo fading out1PublishRemix
